---
title: "get_top_ten"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `get_top_ten()`

Create a Daily Top 10 Table for a Date Range

## Usage

```r
get_top_ten(
  token = "FLIX_PATROL",
  platform_name,
  country_name,
  start_date,
  end_date,
  flix_type
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `token` | Character. API environment variable name. |
| `platform_name` | Character. Name of the platform. |
| `country_name` | Character. Name of the country. |
| `start_date` | Date/Character. Start of the range. |
| `end_date` | Date/Character. End of the range. |
| `flix_type` | Character. Content type. |

## Value

A combined tibble for the specified date range.

## Description

Iterates over a sequence of dates to build a historical Top 10 dataset.

## Details

This function generates a date vector between 'start_date' and 'end_date'
and maps 'get_single_top_ten' over each date. The results
are bound together into a single master tibble.
